keyword: best herbal teas for bad breath
intro_1: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, is a common health issue that affects millions of people worldwide every year. It's estimated that around 65% of the population experiences bad breath at some point in their lives, with the prevalence being highest in adolescents and young adults. This problem can negatively affect an individual's quality of life by causing embarrassment, social isolation, and even anxiety. The main causes of bad breath include poor oral hygiene, dental problems, tongue thrust, and systemic issues such as respiratory infections or digestive disorders. Bad breath can also have negative effects on other aspects of health, including an increased risk of gum disease, tooth decay, and cardiovascular disease.
intro_2: Herbal tea is an effective and natural remedy to fight bad breath. It contains several ingredients that work together to freshen up the mouth, kill bacteria and reduce inflammation. Some of the most common herbs used in bad breath teas include mint, parsley, ginger, and fennel. Drinking herbal tea after meals or as a daily routine can help to eliminate odors and promote good oral health. It's also important to note that herbal tea is not a substitute for proper dental care, but it can be used in conjunction with brushing, flossing, and regular visits to the dentist to keep your mouth healthy and fresh.
title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath
intro: Herbal teas, particularly those made from ingredients like cloves, cinnamon, and peppermint, can help with bad breath due to their antibacterial properties that reduce the number of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The warm temperature of the tea can also stimulate saliva production, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria. Additionally, some herbal teas, such as chamomile and lemon balm, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe irritated gums and reduce inflammation in the mouth. Furthermore, the strong flavors of some herbal teas can temporarily mask any lingering bad breath, providing a quick and natural solution for freshening breath. Lastly, herbal teas are often caffeine-free and sugar-free, making them a healthier alternative to other breath-freshening options like coffee or sweetened gum.

remedies.remedy_desc: Parsley tea helps with bad breath because it contains chlorophyll, a natural deodorizer that neutralizes the germs and bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. The polyphenols in parsley also inhibit the growth of these odor-causing microorganisms. Furthermore, parsley's high antioxidant properties promote overall oral health by reducing inflammation and helping to prevent gum disease. Additionally, drinking hot teas like parsley tea can increase saliva production, which helps wash away food particles and bacteria. Lastly, as a natural herb, parsley does not have the same drying effect as mint-based products, ensuring that your breath stays fresh for longer periods.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Parsley leaves are rich in chlorophyll, which helps neutralize bad breath causes by oral bacteria.
Seeds: Parsley seeds contain essential oils with antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath.
Stems: Parsley stems have a high concentration of flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria.
Roots: Parsley roots contain a compound called myristicin, which has been shown to have antimicrobial effects that can help freshen breath.
remedy_constituents: Chlorophyll: Known for its potent deodorizing properties, chlorophyll helps neutralize bad breath caused by gut-related issues and oral bacteria.
Apigenin: This antioxidant flavonoid contributes to oral health by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms responsible for bad breath.
Myristicin: With natural anti-microbial properties, myristicin combats oral bacteria, reducing the source of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
Limonene: As a natural compound with a pleasant citrus scent, limonene helps mask and neutralize bad breath, leaving a fresher sensation in the mouth.
Alpha-pinene: This constituent, also found in pine needles, provides natural antibacterial properties, aiding in the reduction of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin exhibits antimicrobial properties, suppressing the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, place one tightly packed cup of fresh parsley leaves (preferably Italian flat-leaf variety) and pour 4 cups of boiling water over them.
Cover the teapot and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract into the water.
Strain the tea into cups, discarding the spent parsley leaves, and optionally add a touch of honey or lemon juice for flavor if desired.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature, ideally reaching lukewarm or close to room temperature.
Sip the parsley tea slowly, swishing it around in your mouth before swallowing, to ensure all areas of your mouth receive its refreshing and breath-freshening benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with bad breath because it contains active compounds called chamazulene and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These components help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, creating an unfriendly environment for bacteria that cause bad breath. Furthermore, the soothing effect of chamomile tea on the mucous membranes can help alleviate irritations in the mouth and throat, preventing the overproduction of foul-smelling volatile sulfur compounds. Additionally, chamomile tea's mild astringent qualities help tighten and contract oral tissues, reducing the formation of microscopic pockets where anaerobic bacteria can thrive. Lastly, chamomile tea's pleasant taste and calming effects often encourage individuals to drink it more frequently, thus promoting saliva production and maintaining a cleaner, fresher oral environment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The chamomile flowers are used for medicinal tea due to their natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce bad breath caused by bacteria in the mouth.
Leaves: Chamomile leaves are rich in flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them useful for treating bad breath caused by infections or inflammation.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds contain essential oils that have calming and soothing effects on the stomach and digestive system, helping to reduce bad breath caused by digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Chamazulene: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce oral inflammation and eliminate bad breath caused by bacteria.
Bisabolol: With its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antimicrobial properties, bisabolol helps to decrease inflammation and fight the bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Matricin: As a precursor to chamazulene, matricin also possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, aiding in reducing bacteria-induced bad breath.
Apigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of microorganisms responsible for bad breath.
Luteolin: Luteolin is another flavonoid found in chamomile, exhibiting antibacterial and antioxidant activities, which can help combat bad breath by inhibiting bacterial growth and oxidation.
remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling (212°F) for 1-2 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser to prevent over-steeping and cool down the tea.
Sip and swish the chamomile tea in your mouth for 30 seconds before swallowing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that combat the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The tea's aromatic compounds, such as fenchone and anethole, also help to neutralize foul odors. Furthermore, fennel tea stimulates saliva production, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria stuck between teeth. Additionally, it freshens breath by leaving a lasting, pleasant taste in the mouth. Regular consumption of fennel tea can promote overall oral health, thus preventing bad breath.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in mouth-freshening essential oils and help eliminate bad breath by inhibiting the growth of bacteria in the mouth.
Leaves: The aromatic leaves of fennel have antimicrobial properties that aid in killing oral bacteria, which can help reduce bad breath.
Stems: Fennel stems contain compounds that promote saliva production, which helps wash away odor-causing food particles and bacteria in the mouth.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are a mild natural anti-inflammatory, reducing inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can lessen bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, the main component of fennel's volatile oil, has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Fenchone: Fenchone, another component of fennel's essential oil, also has antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the bacterial load in the mouth and freshen breath.
Limonene: Limonene, found in fennel's essential oil, has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which may help control the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Menthol: Menthol, a compound also present in fennel, provides a cooling sensation and helps freshen breath by reducing oral bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Thoroughly rinse 1 tablespoon of fennel seeds, then crush them using a mortar and pestle.
In a small saucepan, combine the crushed fennel seeds with 2 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the saucepan, and let the tea simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the fennel tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Sip the warm tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and throat, and maintain fresh breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mint tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can kill or slow the growth of bacteria and viruses in the mouth that cause bad breath. The menthol present in mint tea can help to freshen breath by providing a strong, pleasant scent, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the gums and mouth. Additionally, mint tea can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to rinse away food particles and bacteria that can lead to bad breath. Furthermore, drinking mint tea regularly can help to prevent the buildup of plaque on the teeth, which is a primary cause of bad breath. Lastly, mint tea is a natural and caffeine-free alternative to other breath freshening products, making it a healthy and effective way to maintain fresh breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, leaf extracts can help reduce the growth of bacteria causing bad breath.
Flowers: Containing high amounts of rosmarinic acid, flowers can help eliminate oral bacteria and freshen breath.
Seeds: High in essential oils, the seeds can be used to make a soothing tea that helps alleviate inflammation and eliminate bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: This compound gives mint its refreshing taste and smell, and can help to neutralize the bacteria and volatile sulfur compounds responsible for bad breath.
Menthone: This mint constituent has antimicrobial properties that can help to kill off the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Limonene: This compound, found in mint, has been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help to reduce the amount of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Carvone: This constituent of mint has been found to have antimicrobial and antifungal effects that can help to reduce bad breath caused by bacteria and fungus.
Eucalyptol: This compound, also known as cineole, can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat and has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help to freshen breath.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 cup of fresh mint leaves with 2 cups of water and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a separate pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 teaspoon of lemon juice to the boiling water.
Combine the boiling water mixture with the steeped mint leaves.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cardamom tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which are often the root cause of bad breath. The spice's volatile oils, including cineole, terpineol, and limonene, have the ability to neutralize odor by killing germs and freshening breath. Furthermore, cardamom's strong aroma can overpower and mask the unpleasant smell of bad breath, providing instant relief. Additionally, its potent anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, promoting better oral health and preventing bad breath. Lastly, cardamom stimulates saliva production, which helps wash away food particles and bacteria, keeping the mouth clean and fresh.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Cardamom seeds are rich in cineole, a compound with natural antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth causing bad breath.
Flowers: Cardamom flowers contain essential oils that can freshen breath and promote oral health.
Leaves: Cardamom leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat bad breath due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds of the cardamom plant are a potent source of antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and eliminate the causes of bad breath.
Stems: Cardamom stems contain compounds that can kill the bacteria responsible for bad breath and promote fresh breath.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This compound has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria causing bad breath.
Limonene: With its strong aroma, limonene helps to freshen breath and overpower unpleasant odors.
Alpha-terpinyl acetate: This constituent provides a sweet, floral aroma that contributes to cardamom's breath-freshening effects.
1,8-Cineole: This compound has been shown to have antiseptic properties, helping to kill bacteria that cause bad breath.
Linalyl acetate: This compound's pleasing aroma helps to mask bad breath odors, while its potential anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums.
Methyl eugenol: This constituent has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation in the mouth that can contribute to bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Crush 4 cardamom pods to release their seeds, then grind the seeds to a fine powder (2-3 minutes).
Bring 4 cups of water to a boil, add 1 teaspoon of the ground cardamom seeds and reduce heat to a simmer (5 minutes).
Stir in 1 tablespoon of black tea leaves, continue simmering to allow the flavors to infuse (3-4 minutes).
Remove from heat, mix in 2 teaspoons of honey and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Strain the tea into cups, sipping it slowly throughout the day to keep bad breath at bay.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with bad breath because it contains cinnamaldehyde, an organic compound that fights the growth of bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath. Additionally, cinnamon tea's antibacterial and antimicrobial properties inhibit the development of oral microbes, reducing the intensity of unpleasant mouth odors. Furthermore, the essential oils found in cinnamon, such as eugenol, can act as a natural mouth freshener, leaving a pleasant and long-lasting scent. Moreover, cinnamon tea's astringent qualities help tighten and contract oral tissue, which may limit the spaces where bacteria can thrive. Lastly, drinking cinnamon tea promotes saliva production, which washes away food particles, neutralizes acids, and maintains a balanced pH level in the mouth, thus preventing bad breath.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon plant is used in medicinal teas for bad breath due to its antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are used in medicinal teas for bad breath because they contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help promote oral health.

Buds: The buds of the cinnamon plant are used in medicinal teas for bad breath thanks to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils, which can help eliminate bad breath and maintain fresh breath.
Seeds: Cinnamon seeds are used in medicinal teas for bad breath due to their potent antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help eliminate oral bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound in cinnamon has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Eugenol: With its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, eugenol helps decrease oral inflammation and inhibit the growth of odor-causing microorganisms.
Linalool: This compound exhibits antibacterial properties, which help control bacterial growth that contributes to bad breath.
Methyl chavicol: With its antimicrobial effects, methyl chavicol inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for halitosis.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 cinnamon stick or 1 teaspoon of cinnamon powder, and reduce heat to a simmer.
Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the cinnamon to release its oils.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the cinnamon solids.
Optional: Add 1 teaspoon of honey or a squeeze of lemon for added flavor, and sip the tea slowly to freshen breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with bad breath because it contains natural oils that aid in neutralizing foul odors caused by bacteria in the mouth. The antimicrobial properties of ginger inhibit the growth of microorganisms that contribute to bad breath, providing a clean and fresh feeling. Furthermore, ginger stimulates saliva production, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that may lead to bad breath. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce gum inflammation and promote better oral health, ultimately reducing the likelihood of bad breath. Lastly, ginger's strong, aromatic scent can temporarily mask any residual odors, leaving the mouth smelling fresh and pleasant.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root of the ginger plant is rich in gingerol, which helps to neutralize foul odors and promote fresh breath.
Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes have antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria causing bad breath, providing a natural solution to the problem.
Leaves: Ginger leaves contain high levels of chlorophyll, which has been shown to help reduce bad breath by neutralizing odor-causing compounds.
Flowers: Ginger flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, preventing bad breath caused by inflammation.
Seeds: Ginger seeds contain a compound called shogaol, which has been shown to have antibacterial and antimicrobial properties, making it effective at killing the bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, the primary active compounds in ginger, have potent antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath.
Shogaols: Shogaols, another important compound found in ginger, exhibit strong anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the mouth and helping to prevent bad breath caused by inflammation.
Paradols: Paradols, a type of phenolic compound in ginger, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful oral bacteria, helping to freshen breath and maintain oral hygiene.

Zingerone: Zingerone, a compound formed when ginger is cooked or dried, has antioxidant and antibacterial properties, helping to eliminate bad breath by neutralizing harmful bacteria and reducing inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 inch of fresh ginger, then steep in hot water for 5 minutes to extract the flavor and beneficial compounds.
Add 1 teabag of green or black tea, if desired, to the ginger water for added antioxidants and a more well-rounded flavor.
Steep the tea for an additional 3-5 minutes, depending on preferred strength.
Remove the tea bag and ginger pieces, then sweeten the tea with 1-2 teaspoons of honey for its natural antibacterial properties.
Sip the ginger tea slowly, allowing it to coat the mouth and throat, and help neutralize bad breath causing bacteria.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cloves tea helps with bad breath because it contains eugenol, a potent antibacterial agent that kills the microorganisms in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath. Additionally, the compound's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce irritation in the gums and mouth, providing an overall refreshing and soothing effect. Moreover, cloves tea's warm and comforting nature encourages saliva production, which naturally washes away food particles and bacteria that can lead to bad breath. The tea's aromatic and robust flavor also leaves a pleasant and long-lasting aftertaste, further contributing to its breath-freshening benefits. Furthermore, cloves tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the gums and teeth from damage, promoting overall oral health and contributing to fresh and clean breath.
remedy_parts: Buds: Cloves' buds are used in medicinal tea to freshen breath due to their potent essential oil, eugenol, which has antibacterial properties.
Stems: The stems of cloves contain flavonoids that work with eugenol to kill oral bacteria, helping to prevent bad breath.
Seeds: Cloves' seeds are a great addition to medicinal tea for bad breath because they have high antioxidant levels, reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting bacterial growth.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: This compound is the primary active constituent in cloves, and its potent antibacterial properties help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Methyl salicylate: This organic compound found in cloves has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the redness and irritation in the gums, which can contribute to bad breath.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpene present in cloves helps to protect the gums from harmful bacteria, reducing the risk of gum diseases like periodontitis, which can cause bad breath.

Flavonoids: Cloves have several types of flavonoids that have antioxidant properties and work as anti-inflammatory agents that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth caused by harmful bacteria, thereby improving breath.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of whole cloves and reduce heat to low, simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off heat, cover, and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove cloves, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Sip the cloves tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your mouth and freshen your breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anise tea helps with bad breath because it contains anethole, a compound that gives it a distinct licorice-like flavor and potent antimicrobial properties. These antimicrobial effects help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for causing bad breath, particularly those that break down food particles and produce volatile sulfur compounds. Furthermore, anise tea's antibacterial properties combat oral infections and inflammation, both of which can contribute to halitosis. Additionally, anise tea promotes the production of saliva, which aids in washing away food debris and bacteria, thus creating an unfavorable environment for microbial growth and bad breath. Lastly, anise tea's soothing qualities can help alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, that might otherwise exacerbate bad breath.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Anise seeds are rich in anethole, a compound with antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth causing bad breath.
Leaves: Anise leaves, with their aromatic flavor, can help freshen breath and have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues that can contribute to bad breath.
Flowers: Anise flowers contain essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, making them a useful addition to medicinal tea to combat bad breath caused by bacteria.
Rhizomes: Anise rhizomes have a similar chemical composition to the seeds, with anethole being the primary compound, and have been used in traditional medicine to treat breath odor.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is the primary active ingredient in anise, and its antibacterial properties help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Methyl chavicol: Methyl chavicol, also known as estragole, has antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce the amount of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Anisaldehyde: Anisaldehyde, another important compound in anise, has been shown to have antibacterial and anti-fungal properties, which can help to freshen breath.
Limonene: Limonene, a compound found in the essential oil of anise, has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to kill bacteria and reduce bad breath.
Acetoacetanilide: Acetoacetanilide, a chemical compound found in anise, has been used in some breath fresheners due to its ability to neutralize unpleasant odors.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of anise seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the anise seeds.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and freshen your breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that reduce the growth of oral bacteria, which are a major cause of bad breath. The compound rosmarinic acid in sage inhibits the production of foul-smelling substances released by bacteria. Moreover, sage's astringent qualities help to tighten gum tissues, reducing areas where bacteria can thrive. Additionally, sage tea's antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy oral tissues. Lastly, its pleasant flavor and soothing effects can encourage regular sipping, ensuring long-lasting oral freshness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant are often used in medicinal teas due to their potent antibacterial properties, which can help to eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.

Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the gums and eliminating the causes of bad breath.
Flowers: The flowers of the sage plant contain essential oils that have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to kill off the bacteria that cause bad breath.
Seeds: The seeds of the sage plant are a great source of essential oils and acids, which can help to neutralize the odors that cause bad breath and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: This compound has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath.
Camphor: Camphor's antimicrobial properties aid in reducing the growth of odor-causing microorganisms in the mouth and throat.
Eucalyptol: This compound has been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to freshen breath by reducing the number of bacteria in the mouth.
Rosmarinic acid: With its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, rosmarinic acid can help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Carnosic acid: This compound has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help protect the gums and teeth, reducing the risk of gum disease and related bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling point (around 2-3 minutes in a microwave or 4-5 minutes on a stovetop).
Crush 3-4 fresh sage leaves, releasing their aroma, and add them to the hot water.
Allow the sage leaves to steep in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove sage leaves, then let the tea cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Sip and swish the warm sage tea in your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute, repetitively, before swallowing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that kill the bacteria causing bad breath. Additionally, rosemary is a natural deodorizer that neutralizes the unpleasant odors in the mouth. Its high concentration of chlorogenic acid also fights plaque buildup, preventing the formation of volatile sulfur compounds that result in bad breath. Moreover, rosemary tea's astringent qualities tighten gum tissues, reducing the chances of food particles getting trapped and causing foul breath. Lastly, rosemary's strong aroma, similar to camphor and pine needles, leaves a fresh and pleasant scent in the mouth long after drinking the tea.
